revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23410164509|commit triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Fake_heading%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation subpage}} {{ombox|type=content|text=Don't use this template in articles. See [[WP:PSEUDOHEAD]]}} {{template shortcut|fhead|Fake header|Fake title|Fakeheader|Fakeheading}} {{TemplateStyles|Template:Fake heading/styles.css}} ==Usage== This template is used to create examples of section heading [[Help:Wiki markup|wiki markup]] in help and similar pages and should [[WP:PSEUDOHEAD|NOT be used in articles]]. Fake headings do not appear in the [[:en:H:TOC|table of contents]]. They are styled to appear the same as the default heading styles in the Vector [[WP:Skin|skin]], and will not be influenced by custom [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] or skins. ==Parameters== * {{para|level}} or {{para|sub}} is the level number: valid values are 1 through 6. The default is 2, equivalent of a == Main heading ==. Level 1 is usually reserved for the page's title. * An unnamed parameter (which can also be specified as {{para|1}}) is used for the section title. ==Example== {{markup | {{fake heading|sub=1|Heading 1}} {{fake heading|sub=2|Heading 2}} {{fake heading|sub=3|Heading 3}} {{fake heading|sub=4|Heading 4}} {{fake heading|sub=5|Heading 5}} {{fake heading|sub=6|Heading 6}} | {{fake heading|sub=1|Heading 1}} {{fake heading|sub=2|Heading 2}} {{fake heading|sub=3|Heading 3}} {{fake heading|sub=4|Heading 4}} {{fake heading|sub=5|Heading 5}} {{fake heading|sub=6|Heading 6}} }} == See also == * {{Tl|Heading}} {{fake template list}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Documentation assistance templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
